summary: The run sheet is ready for orchestration. One coordinator owns one ignored feat/aws-ecs-program worktree, serializes all mutations, closes slices on truthful program-branch anchors, preserves the Plan 02 to 06 to 07 lock chain, runs the full suite only in final washup, and fast-forwards the exact tested candidate into release/0.7.1 before GO.

review_basis.head: b50b5da62a2fee91128e9993df0828f50feaf8fc
review_basis.process: Independent xhigh reality, architecture, quality/security, and systems re-review followed by source-diff inspection, stale-term search, checksum, link, JSON, shell-syntax, workflow-trigger, tracker-path, live-DAG, critical-path, and final-fast-forward checks.

warnings.detail: Later stages still require named operator authority, Docker, hosted CI, AWS credentials and infrastructure, protected evidence storage, and cleanup capacity; absence is an explicit stop condition.
Planning approval is not AWS runtime approval. Only Plan 12 may return GO for one exact source SHA, image digest, and platform after live proof and cleanup.

resolved_findings.resolution: The exact 20-node live DAG, direct prerequisites, critical path, conservative stage barriers, and serialized shared-surface ownership are machine-checked.
The run sheet supersedes duplicate plan-local claim, worktree, branch, and close mechanics while retaining every implementation, test, evidence, staging, and commit checkbox.
Plan 12 runs Tasks 1 through 8 on one frozen feat/aws-ecs-program candidate; Task 9 alone fast-forwards the paused release branch to that exact tested SHA before GO.
Every plan is checksum-bound to its review sidecar; Plan 12 repeats that gate immediately before closeout so intentional downstream plan edits cannot consume stale approval.
Slice and integrated evidence use closed schemas requiring zero exit, zero skips, full collected-test accounting, and an envelope bound to the current shared program tip.

Fresh and interrupted work use exact-owner historical claim anchors, recover partial setup without abandoning WIP, heartbeat long leases, and close only after focused program-tip verification and anchor revalidation.

Plan 10 atomically claims before source qualification, records one idempotent qualified-baseline receipt, preserves the historical baseline through its own commits, and hard-stops post-integration lineage reconstruction without owner approval.

Reopen handling derives transitive descendants and affected phases from the refreshed live graph, reopens closed descendants in reverse topological order, invalidates their evidence, and re-executes topologically.

verification: all 18 dated program plans exist in docs/superpowers/plans/aws with 18 matching review sidecars
every plan_file exists and all 18 plan_sha256 values match
all Markdown code fences are balanced
all 56 Bash or shell fences in the five reviewed sources pass bash -n
no pre-relocation AWS plan path remains in repository or live milestone data
live Filigree milestone contains exactly 20 expected steps and every direct dependency matches
live Plan 03B tracker scope owns Tasks 3-4 and the complete handoff
read-only descendant derivation produces a valid reverse-topological worklist and affected-phase set

review_history.summary: Initial orchestration reviews found duplicate claim/worktree/close mechanics, an invalid Plan 12 feature-worktree path, pre-qualification Plan 10 baseline recording, stale review/evidence risks, and incomplete descendant reopen handling.
